This authentic Celine Trio Crossbody Leather Small in hot pink is a playful minimalist bag to carry for on-the-go moments.
Made in supple, bright pink calfskin leather, this petite crossbody is accented with gold tonal snap buttons that easily detached the three pouches converting the bag into singular clutches.
The adjustable shoulder strap creates easy functionality synonymous to Celine.
The Celine logo is imprinted discreetly at the front of the bag in gold and personifies Celine's minimalist, chic style.
Authenticity code .
**Note: Shoe photographed is used as a sizing reference, and does not come with the bag.

Condition: Very good.
Light signs of wear on exterior.
Peeling on base of strap.

The Selenga Pulp & Paper Company operates a zero discharge unbleached Kraft Mill.
Located in an enviromnentally sensitive area, the operation is situated on the Selenga River, about  kilometers from the south shore of Lake Baikal.
The Selenga River is the largest source of water for Lake Baikal, a lake considered by Russians as a natural resource jewel.
Lake Baikal is one of the worlds deepest and cleanest lakes and contains one fifth of the worlds fresh water, more water than the Great Lakes combined.
Until , the Selenga Pulp & Paper Co. was considered one of the major polluters of the river.
However, in August , the mill began effluent recycling.
This was accomplished by activated sludge technology in conjunction with chemical - physical treatment.
The mill, in partnership with Sibgiprobum, a design institute for the Siberian pulp and paper industry developed the closed cycle system.
The regulatory history, and treatment plant design will be reviewed in this paper.

CVEK is an independent non-governmental organisation that strives to contribute to building a more just and cohesive society through research, analytical and educational activities.
The Centre was founded in  and is based in Bratislava.
Their common mission is to make a systematic effort aimed at creating an environment in which every person has every right to feel like a valuable and respected human being.
Their work is rooted in defending fundamental human rights principles such as respecting dignity of every person and promoting diversity of society.
The activities of CVEK focus particularly on ethnic, language and religious minorities.
Thematically, they place emphasis especially on education, housing, social status, interethnic relations and political participation.
Their activities may be divided into three basic pillars: research, advocacy and education.
They specialise in promoting inclusion of marginalised population groups, be it the Roma, migrants or other minorities.

Keeping up to date with changing accounting standards and presentational requirements can be a headache, especially when you only dip in to it once a year.
Well, Hayhursts offer a full statutory accounts production service, we'll take responsibility - and that headache - and ensure your annual accounts are complete, on time, and meet with all current standards and practises.
We'll even ensure the relevant copies are sent to all those concerned.
We won't miss a beat.
Armed with many, many years' experience, we can produce your statutory accounts from your own records or from our bookkeeping service and use market leading software; regularly updated to ensure full compliance with the latest standards and regulations.
We will also provide the accounts in iXBRL format for accompanying the corporation tax return.
For more information on the range of accounting services we provide please contact us.

EU citizen who fought for terrorists was detained in Ilovaisk - Life in Ukraine.
Live, @ first hand.
EU citizen of Serbian orgin has been detained in Eastern Ukraine in the town of Ilovaisk.
The detaned man has the nationality of Slovak Republic, he was born in Banská Bystrica.
This was reported by Advisor to the Minister of Internal Affairs Anton Gerashchenko.
'He was mortar man [when fought for] terrorists.
He said that he'd allegedly come to Ukraine not as a mercenary but due to his convictions.
This version will be checked by investigation'.
It was also informed that the detainee had a passport of Slovakian citizen with a note of crossing of Ukrainian border on Jully ,  in Julyiany airport.
'This is the first documented capturing of EU citizen fighting on the side of terrorists', - Gerashchenko said.
Slovakian citizen said that he knew that there were another citizens of his county fighting for terrorists, but at that moment he didn't know whether they had survived after the night assault.
Share the post "EU citizen who fought for terrorists was detained in Ilovaisk"

Rupert Shortt, author of "Christianophobia: A Faith Under Attack," has a good piece in London's Evening Standard newspaper, pointing out the hypocrisy so evident in commentary on the Middle East.
That so often the West is blamed for Islamophobia, or even for many of the tragic events of the region.
Yet the residents themselves do not recognize their own complicity in the intense and widespread persecution of Christians.
The standard replies to views as one-sided as these are familiar.
I took a different line, reminding my driver that overall, Christians - starting with more than a million church members butchered by government forces in Sudan - have endured far worse treatment from Muslims than vice versa in recent decades but have largely kept quiet about it.
When I pointed out that there is scarcely a country between Morocco and Pakistan in which Christians are fully free to worship without harassment, the cabbie eyed me with bewilderment.
This entry was posted in Middle East, Persecution on August ,  by admin.

In February , the EEOC district office in New York, New York filed this suit against Marchfirst, Inc. and USweb/CKS in the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York.
The EEOC alleged that the defendants violated Title VII of the Civil Rights Act by demoting, disciplining, and constructively discharging an executive assistant because she was pregnant.
The charging party moved to intervene in the case, but before the court ruled on the motion, the case was settled.
In March  a consent decree was entered; the decree is unavailable, but the EEOC litigation report states that the defendant agreed to pay the complainant $.

With the dawn of virtualization and Infrastructureas-a-Service (IaaS), the comprehensive technical computing community is in view of the use of clouds for their technical computing needs.
This is due to the relative scalability, ease of use, advanced user milieu customization abilities clouds provide, as well as many novel computing archetypes available for data-intensive applications.
However, there is still a notable gap that exists between the performances of IaaS when compared to typical high performance computing (HPC) resources, limiting the applicability of IaaS for many potential scientific users.
Most newly, general-purpose graphics processing units(GPGPUs or GPUs) have become commonplace within high performance supercomputers.
We propose to bridge the gap between supercomputing and Clouds by providing GPUenabled virtual machines.
Specifically, the Xen hypervisor is utilized to leverage specialized hardware-assisted I/O virtualization tools in order to provide advanced HPC-centric Nvidia GPUs directly in guest VMs.
We evaluate this work by measuring the performance of two Nvidia Tesla GPUs and comparing to bare-metal hardware.
Results show this method of leveraging GPUs within virtual machines is a viable use case for many scientific computing workflows, and could help support high performance cloud infrastructure in the near future.

In February  the Special Interest Group for Ocular Tissue Transplantion was initiated and established by the Netherlands Institute for Innovative Ocular Surgery (NIIOS) to promote the exchange of information, cooperation and innovation in the field of ocular tissue transplantation.
In the last decades, there have been a lot of developments in the field of tissue transplantation.
The specialists of NIIOS experience both internationally and locally an increasing demand to share knowledge and to jointly bring innovations to a higher level.
This applies not only to the field of clinical ophthalmology, but also to other specialties involved in tissue transplantation.
In addition information exchange and collaboration among different tissue banks involved in the whole process from tissue harvest to tissue transplantation can contribute to innovation.
Depending on the initiatives and interests of SIGOTT members the objectives may be broadened.
